core_loop.one_sentence: Drag and drop units into your castle to automatically fire at and destroy the enemy's castle before yours falls.
core_loop.player_goal: Destroy the enemy castle by reducing its health to 0%.
core_loop.failure_or_pressure: The enemy castle continuously fires back, reducing your castle's health.
core_loop.why_it_is_fun: Satisfying destruction physics and the strategic placement of units to maximize damage.

player_controls.target: Unit cards at the bottom to empty slots in the player's castle
player_controls.result: Places the unit, which then begins auto-firing at the enemy.

visible_ui.purpose: Show the remaining health percentage of both castles.
Allows the player to select which units to deploy.

timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Establishes the conflict and the threat of the enemy.

timeline_beats.playable_relevance: The primary action the user will take in the playable.
Shows the consequence of not acting fast enough or making the wrong choices.

mechanics.name: Drag-and-Drop Deployment
mechanics.description: Moving units from a UI panel into specific slots in the game world.

mechanics.name: Auto-Battling
mechanics.description: Deployed units automatically target and fire at the enemy.

mechanics.name: Destructible Environments
mechanics.description: Castles visually break apart as they take damage.

feedback_and_juice.visual_or_audio_feedback: Explosion particles, smoke, screen shake, flying debris, damage numbers.
Unit disappears, replaced by a 'RIP' tombstone.

can_simplify: Complex physics-based destruction (use sprite swapping for damage states instead)
Multiple unit types (stick to one for simplicity)

variation_hooks.effect: Changes how quickly the player can win.

open_questions_or_assumptions: Do different slots in the castle provide different advantages (e.g., range)?
Are the projectile trajectories physics-based or just visual animations?
